instructions.instruction: Use specific version tags — NEVER use :latest as the base image tag
instructions.original_snippets: Use specific version tags (NEVER use :latest)...FROM node:latest resolves to a different image on each build...GOOD: FROM node:20.18-alpine3.20
instructions.relevant_when: Whenever writing any FROM instruction in a Dockerfile

instructions.instruction: Use multi-stage builds — separate build stage from runtime stage, keep build tools out of final image
instructions.original_snippets: Multi-Stage Builds (REQUIRED for compiled languages, RECOMMENDED for all): Separate build stage from runtime stage. Keep build tools out of final image. Copy only necessary artifacts.
instructions.relevant_when: When containerizing any application, especially compiled languages (Go, Java, TypeScript/Node.js builds)

instructions.instruction: Run as non-root user — create a dedicated non-root user and use USER directive before CMD
instructions.original_snippets: Run as non-root user (create dedicated user)...NEVER run containers as root...BAD: No USER instruction (defaults to root). GOOD: RUN addgroup -S appgroup && adduser -S appuser -G appgroup followed by USER appuser
instructions.relevant_when: In every Dockerfile for any application

instructions.instruction: Use minimal base images (alpine, distroless) — not full OS images
instructions.original_snippets: Use minimal base images (alpine, distroless)...Node.js: ~50-150MB with Alpine (vs ~1GB with full node image)...Go: ~5-20MB with distroless/scratch
instructions.relevant_when: When choosing base images for any language

instructions.instruction: Copy dependency files before application code (COPY package*.json ./ before COPY . .)
instructions.original_snippets: Copy dependency files before application code...NEVER copy the entire build context before installing dependencies...BAD: COPY . . followed by RUN npm ci. GOOD: COPY package*.json ./ then RUN npm ci then COPY . .
instructions.relevant_when: Whenever installing dependencies (npm, pip, go mod, maven, etc.)

instructions.instruction: Combine related RUN commands with && and clean up package manager caches in the same layer
instructions.original_snippets: Combine related RUN commands with &&...Clean up package manager caches in same layer...NEVER split related package installation across multiple RUN layers...GOOD: RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
instructions.relevant_when: When writing RUN instructions that install system packages or dependencies

instructions.instruction: Use COPY not ADD when no archive extraction or URL fetch is needed
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER use ADD when COPY is sufficient...ADD has implicit behaviors (auto-extracting tarballs, fetching URLs) that make Dockerfiles harder to reason about; COPY is explicit and predictable...BAD: ADD app.tar.gz /app/. GOOD: COPY app.tar.gz /app/
instructions.relevant_when: Whenever copying files into the image

instructions.instruction: Add HEALTHCHECK for services
instructions.original_snippets: Add HEALTHCHECK for services...Production Readiness (REQUIRED): Add HEALTHCHECK for services
instructions.relevant_when: When containerizing long-running web services or APIs

instructions.instruction: Use exec form for ENTRYPOINT/CMD (JSON array syntax, not shell string)
instructions.original_snippets: Use exec form for ENTRYPOINT/CMD...Production Readiness (REQUIRED): Use exec form for ENTRYPOINT/CMD
instructions.relevant_when: When writing CMD or ENTRYPOINT instructions

instructions.instruction: Set WORKDIR to absolute paths
instructions.original_snippets: Set WORKDIR to absolute paths...Production Readiness (REQUIRED): Set WORKDIR to absolute paths
instructions.relevant_when: In any Dockerfile that sets a working directory

instructions.instruction: Document exposed ports with EXPOSE
instructions.original_snippets: Document exposed ports with EXPOSE...Production Readiness (REQUIRED): Document exposed ports with EXPOSE
instructions.relevant_when: When containerizing any network service that listens on a port

instructions.instruction: Always include syntax directive: # syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
instructions.original_snippets: Always Include: Syntax directive: # syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
instructions.relevant_when: In every Dockerfile generated

instructions.instruction: Always create .dockerignore alongside the Dockerfile
instructions.original_snippets: Always create .dockerignore with generated Dockerfile...Generate .dockerignore: Objective: Create comprehensive .dockerignore to reduce build context and prevent secret leaks.
instructions.relevant_when: Every time a Dockerfile is generated

instructions.instruction: .dockerignore must exclude: .git, .env/.env.*, .github, Dockerfile*, docker-compose*.yml, node_modules/, IDE directories
instructions.original_snippets: # Git .git .gitignore ... # Environment .env .env.* ... # Dependencies node_modules/ __pycache__/ ... # IDE .vscode/ .idea/
instructions.relevant_when: When generating .dockerignore for any project

instructions.instruction: No hardcoded secrets in ENV instructions
instructions.original_snippets: No hardcoded secrets...Security Hardening (REQUIRED): No hardcoded secrets
instructions.relevant_when: When writing ENV instructions or handling environment variables

instructions.instruction: Order Dockerfile instructions from least to most frequently changing (for cache optimization)
instructions.original_snippets: Order instructions from least to most frequently changing...Layer Optimization (REQUIRED): Order instructions from least to most frequently changing
instructions.relevant_when: When structuring any Dockerfile's instruction sequence

instructions.instruction: Provide Optimization Metrics table in the final output (image size estimate, cache strategy, security summary)
instructions.original_snippets: Optimization Metrics (REQUIRED - provide explicit estimates)...Always include a summary like this: | Metric | Estimate | | Image Size | ~150MB... | Build Cache | ... | Security | ...
instructions.relevant_when: After generating any Dockerfile

instructions.instruction: Provide Next Steps as a bulleted checklist after generation
instructions.original_snippets: Next Steps (REQUIRED - always include as bulleted list)...Always provide explicit next steps: - [ ] Test the build locally...
instructions.relevant_when: After delivering the final Dockerfile

instructions.instruction: Gather requirements before generating (language/version, app type, framework, entry point, ports, env vars, health check)
instructions.original_snippets: Stage 1: Gather Requirements...Information to Collect (use AskUserQuestion if missing or unclear): Language/version, application type, framework, entry point...Port(s), environment variables, config files, health check endpoint
instructions.relevant_when: When the user's request is ambiguous or missing key details

instructions.instruction: Always validate after generation using devops-skills:dockerfile-validator, and iterate at least once if errors are found
instructions.original_snippets: Stage 5: Validate with devops-skills:dockerfile-validator...REQUIRED: Always validate after generation...Stage 6: Iterate on Validation Errors...REQUIRED: Iterate at least ONCE if validation finds errors
instructions.relevant_when: After generating any Dockerfile
